manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: flute, pass, stock, boot, star, tail, kid, line, net.
Your opponent's team words are: wind, arm, sock, jam, jack, slip, spine, lawyer.
Distractor words are: capital, lemon, germany, racket, fire, hawk, cap.
The kill words are: heart.

message.content:
CLUE: FOOTBALL
TARGETS: boot, pass, line, net, star

We need a clue word that relates to multiple of our words. Could be "concert"? maybe flute and star? Not.

Clue can't contain parts of them. So can't be "stock" etc.

Maybe clue "yard". Could relate to tail? No.

But "football" contains "foot" which is part of "boot"? Not disallowed? The clue cannot contain parts of the listed words. Does "football" contain "boot"? No. Contains "ball". Not in list. Could "foot" be part of "boot"? "boot" is b-oot, but "foot" letters overlapping? Not same substring. Probably fine.

Check opponent: "football" doesn't include any opponent. So clue: FOOTBALL, targets: boot, pass, line, net, star? star maybe less. But still okay.

But we need to ensure clue is single word: FOOTBALL. Provide list of target words we intend the partner to guess. Let's pick boot, pass, line, net, star maybe. That's five. Is "star" connected to football? Could be a star player. Yes.

Thus answer format.
